_note: This is a placeholder. Run `python script.py` or trigger the GitHub Action to populate real articles.

articles.title: AllStar Terminal is ready — trigger the GitHub Action to load live news
articles.summary: Your feed aggregator is deployed. Navigate to Actions → 🔄 Refresh News Feed → Run workflow to fetch your first batch of articles.
articles.body_html:
Welcome to AllStar Terminal.

The dashboard is live, but data.json hasn't been populated with real articles yet.

To load live articles:

  1. Push this repository to GitHub.
  2. Enable GitHub Pages (Settings → Pages → Deploy from branch: main, folder: / (root)).
  3. Go to Actions tab → 🔄 Refresh News Feed → click Run workflow.

After the action completes, refresh this page. Articles will auto-refresh every 4 hours thereafter.

articles.title: Setup Guide: Configure your RSS sources in script.py
articles.summary: Open script.py and update the SOURCES list. For X/Twitter feeds, RSSHub must be self-hosted or a public instance used — check rsshub.app for availability.
articles.body_html:
The SOURCES list in script.py controls what gets fetched. Each source has:

  • id: unique identifier
  • label: display name
  • url: RSS/Atom feed URL
  • column: pulse | facts | geek
  • limit: max articles per source (5–8 recommended)

Twitter/X feeds via RSSHub require a working RSSHub instance. The public instance at rsshub.app may be rate-limited. Consider self-hosting RSSHub via Docker for reliability.

articles.title: Reader Mode: Click any card to open the immersive reader
articles.summary: Every news card opens a full reader panel — no redirects, no trackers, no ads. The full article content is fetched once by Python and stored locally in data.json.
articles.body_html:
The Reader Mode is the centerpiece of AllStar Terminal.

When you click a card, the Python-fetched body_html is rendered in a full-screen glassmorphism overlay — sanitized, tracker-free, and formatted for maximum readability.

External links inside articles open in new tabs. You can also click Open original ↗ in the modal header to visit the source website directly.

Close the reader with Esc, clicking the ✕ button, or clicking outside the panel.
